channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Philippines says China's coast guard fired water cannons and sideswiped its patrol vessel
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard vessels backed by navy ships fired powerful water cannons and blocked and sideswiped a Philippine patrol vessel on Wednesday in renewed aggression at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, Philippine officials said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New UN aid chief vows 'ruthlessness' to prioritize spending as funding for world's crises shrinks
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The new head of the U.N. humanitarian aid agency says it will be “ruthless” when prioritizing how to spend money, a nod to challenges in fundraising for civilians in war zones like Gaza, Sudan, Syria and Ukraine. Tom Fletcher, a longtime British diplomat who took up the U.N.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A list of mispronounced words provides a retrospective of 2024, from Kamala to Chappell
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and breakout pop star Chappell Roan were among the year's most talked-about people. Their names were also among the most mispronounced.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An ex-officer who died in an apparent suicide before his abuse trial was not supposed to have a gun
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A former police detective in Kansas who died in an apparent suicide as he was about to stand trial for allegedly sexually assaulting and terrorizing vulnerable women for decades wasn't supposed to have a gun while he was under house arrest.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about sinkholes after a Pennsylvania woman may have disappeared into one
item.description: Authorities fear a grandmother in western Pennsylvania who disappeared while looking for her cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missing Missouri mother found dead, boyfriend charged with murder: police
item.description: Authorities in Missouri announced that a man is behind bars for the murder of his girlfriend, whose body was found in a wooded area, after he had reported her missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: A U.S. official says authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden’s presidency.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Freedom is permanent for Missourian described as the longest-held wrongly incarcerated woman in US
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Years of legal wrangling have come to an end for a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trial begins in the killing of an Ole Miss student who was known in the local LGBTQ+ community
item.description: A University of Mississippi student who disappeared in 2022 was a happy person who spoke with his mother daily and called her every year to sing happy birthday, his mom testified Tuesday in the trial of the man charged with killing her son.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s DEA nominee withdraws from consideration just days after being picked
item.description: President-elect Trump’s choice to lead the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) announced on Tuesday he is withdrawing from consideration just three days after Trump made his nomination known.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Great Lakes region braces for more snow while cleaning up after lake-effect storms
item.description: Even more snow could be on the way for places in the Great Lakes region that are still digging out after days of storms caused deadly wrecks, collapsed a barn on top of 100 cows and buried some towns under nearly 6 feet (1.8 meters).
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado mom accused of killing 2 of her kids fights UK extradition
item.description: Kimberlee Singler is suspected of killing her 9-year-old daughter and 7-year-old son, as well as attempting to kill her 11-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Global warming fills New England's rich waters with death traps for endangered sea turtles
item.description: As global warming fills the plankton-rich waters of New England with death traps for sea turtles, the number of stranded reptiles has multiplied over the last 20 years, filling one specialized animal hospital with the endangered creatures.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Abercrombie CEO's partner pleads not guilty to sex trafficking charges, is freed on bond
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — Matthew Smith, the partner of former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, was released on $10 million bond Tuesday in their sex trafficking case with conditions including home detention and GPS monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California cuts back on water deliveries, anticipating dry year
item.description: The State Water Project is planning for a dry 2025 punctuated by extreme storms like we’ve seen in late November,” Karla Nemeth said,
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok influencer Marlena Velez arrested, accused of theft for 2nd time in weeks: police
item.description: Marlena Velez was previously accused of stealing $500 worth of items from Target and posting incriminating video to social media.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri mother’s fight for justice closes illegal daycare, gets felony conviction
item.description: The mother said the perpetrator “…told all of the kids, ‘If you say something, nobody is going to believe you; you’re a kid. Grown-ups don’t believe kids.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Babies found safe after car stolen and infants dumped in cold, IMPD says
item.description: Shortly before noon, officers located the stolen vehicle. The babies were not inside and the suspect was gone.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Company provides insurance against stolen delivery packages
item.description: PorchPals costs $120 per year, covering up to $2,000 in stolen packages or three claims annually. Monthly billing has a rate of $15.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Community members call for unedited footage in fatal Albuquerque police shooting
item.description: We were there looking for even a shred of accountability, and now over a month later, we are still waiting," said Fred Garcia, Matthew "Solo" Garcia's father.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Honeycutt, NewsNation

item.title: Jon Stewart mocks Dems, Fox News on Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: “The Democrats made this case an example of why Americans should believe in our system," Comedian Jon Stewart said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Daniel Penny jurors begin deliberations in Jordan Neely subway chokehold trial
item.description: Jurors have begun their deliberations in the trial of Daniel Penny, who is charged with manslaughter and criminally negligent homicide for Jordan Neely's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: Jurors have started deliberating in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a man whose behavior was alarming passengers on a New York subway train.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Native American students miss school at higher rates. It only got worse during the pandemic
item.description: SAN CARLOS, Ariz. (AP) — After missing 40 days of school last year, Tommy Betom, 10, is on track this year for much better attendance. The importance of showing up has been stressed repeatedly at school — and at home.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, SHARON LURYE and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The drinking water for New Jersey's capital went largely untested for more than a year, utility says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — The drinking water in New Jersey's capital city and some surrounding communities went largely untested for more than a year, the utility belatedly told roughly 200,000 affected customers, noting that it fired a worker who falsified the relevant reports.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5
